case_studies.title: Titanium Blisk Machining on Aero 5X 750 Cuts Cycle Time by 40%
case_studies.summary: An aerospace OEM replaced a 3+2 setup with a simultaneous 5-axis process on the Aero 5X 750, reducing cycle time by 40% on titanium blisks.
case_studies.content:
A tier-1 aerospace supplier producing titanium blisks for a regional jet engine platform migrated from a legacy 3+2 machining process to a new simultaneous 5-axis strategy on the Aero 5X 750. By leveraging the machine’s 30 kW high-torque spindle and trunnion-style table, the shop consolidated three setups into one, improving positional accuracy and dramatically reducing non-cut time.

Key improvements:
- Cycle time reduced from 11.5 hours to 6.9 hours per blisk
- Surface finish improved from Ra 1.2 µm to Ra 0.6 µm
- Tool life on roughing cutters increased by 25%

The installation included process optimization support, titanium-specific toolpath strategies, and on-site training for programmers and operators.

case_studies.title: Aero 5X 900 Streamlines Titanium Wing Bracket Production
case_studies.summary: Complex titanium wing brackets are now machined in a single clamping on the Aero 5X 900, boosting throughput by 32%.
case_studies.content:
A European aerospace structures manufacturer needed to increase capacity on a family of titanium wing brackets with tight positional tolerances. The Aero 5X 900 provided the working envelope and rigidity required to machine all features in a single clamping.

By utilizing 5-axis swarf milling and optimized roughing strategies, the shop reduced setups from 4 to 1 and eliminated a secondary finishing operation.

Results:
- 32% increase in parts per shift
- Scrap rate reduced from 4.5% to 1.1%
- Cpk values improved on critical holes and interfaces.

case_studies.title: Gantry 5X 1200 Delivers One-Setup Titanium Structural Part Machining
case_studies.summary: A large titanium fuselage frame is machined complete on the Gantry 5X 1200, removing two fixtures and saving 18 hours of setup per batch.
case_studies.content:
An airframe manufacturer struggled with multi-setup machining of a large titanium fuselage frame. With the Gantry 5X 1200, the part is now fully machined in a single setup using long-reach tooling and 5-axis positioning.

The integrated probing system enables in-process verification, reducing manual inspection time and increasing confidence in first-article parts.

case_studies.title: High-Speed Aluminum Pylon Machining on Aero 5X 750
case_studies.summary: High-speed 5-axis machining of aluminum pylons doubles productivity while maintaining tight profile tolerances.
case_studies.content: Using the Aero 5X 750 with a high-speed aluminum spindle setup, an aerospace supplier transitioned from 3-axis roughing and 5-axis finishing to full 5-axis machining. Advanced toolpaths minimized air-cutting and improved chip evacuation.

case_studies.title: VMC 850 Tackles Inconel Engine Mounts with Improved Tool Life
case_studies.summary: Optimized cutting strategies on the VMC 850 deliver 20% better tool life in tough inconel engine brackets.
case_studies.content: A regional engine MRO facility used the VMC 850 to re-machine Inconel engine mounts. Process tuning across speeds, feeds, and coolant delivery yielded a significant reduction in tool wear and a more stable process window.

case_studies.title: ProMill 650 Speeds Up Aluminum Engine Block Roughing
case_studies.summary: Automotive supplier cuts roughing time on aluminum engine blocks by 28% using dynamic milling on the ProMill 650.
case_studies.content: By reprogramming legacy toolpaths to modern dynamic milling strategies, an automotive supplier used the ProMill 650’s 13 kW spindle to dramatically reduce cycle time and improve chip control on aluminum engine blocks.

case_studies.title: MicroMill 400 ATC Supports Lights-Out Titanium Implant Production
case_studies.summary: A medical device startup runs unattended night shifts on MicroMill 400 ATC for small titanium implants.
case_studies.content: The MicroMill 400 ATC, equipped with an automatic tool changer and through-spindle coolant, enabled a medical startup to run night shifts unattended. Using standardized titanium implant fixtures, they achieved consistent quality and reduced labor costs.

case_studies.title: Gantry 5X 1200 Machines Large Turbine Housings in Fewer Setups
case_studies.summary: Energy sector customer reduces setups on large turbine housings from six to two using 5-axis positioning on Gantry 5X 1200.
case_studies.content: An energy OEM leveraged the large travels and 5-axis capabilities of the Gantry 5X 1200 to consolidate machining operations on turbine housings. Integrated probing and custom fixtures allowed precise machining of sealing surfaces and bolt hole patterns.

case_studies.title: ProMill 4X 600 Eliminates Secondary Op on Suspension Brackets
case_studies.summary: Using 4-axis indexing, a Tier-2 automotive supplier removed a secondary drilling operation on steel suspension brackets.
case_studies.content: The ProMill 4X 600’s integrated rotary table allowed the customer to access multiple faces of a complex suspension bracket in one setup. This reduced handling, improved alignment between holes, and increased throughput.

case_studies.title: EcoMill 350 Modernizes Surgical Instrument Production
case_studies.summary: Medical instrument maker replaces manual milling with EcoMill 350 CNC, achieving better repeatability and documentation.
case_studies.content: With the EcoMill 350, a surgical instrument manufacturer automated previously manual and NC-mill operations. The move to CNC reduced variability, simplified validation, and improved traceability of process parameters.
